seoTitle: How to Create Forms and Surveys in Typeform
metaDescription: Learn how to build forms and surveys in Typeform using templates, question customization, logic, and response collection tools.
intro: Typeform's drag-and-drop builder covers both simple forms and more detailed surveys, making it a flexible tool for lead generation, feedback, and research. This guide walks through choosing a template, adding questions, applying logic, and publishing to start collecting responses.

steps.heading: Create a new form or survey
steps.body: Choose a template that fits your goal, whether it's a lead form or a research survey, or start building from a blank canvas.

steps.heading: Add questions and customize design
steps.body: Add the question types you need and adjust the design to match your brand or personal style.

steps.heading: Configure logic and form settings
steps.body: Set up conditional logic and review general settings to control how the form behaves for different respondents.

steps.heading: Publish and collect responses
steps.body: Publish the finished form or survey and share it to start gathering responses from your audience.

faq.question: What's the difference between a form and a survey in Typeform?
faq.answer: Forms and surveys use the same builder, but surveys typically focus more on gathering opinions or feedback, while forms can serve broader purposes like registrations or lead capture.

faq.question: Can I use templates to speed up building?
faq.answer: Yes, Typeform offers templates for common use cases that give you a head start compared to building entirely from scratch.

faq.question: Is logic necessary for every form or survey?
faq.answer: No, logic is optional but useful when you want the questions shown to depend on how someone answers earlier ones.

faq.question: How do I start collecting responses after publishing?
faq.answer: Share the form's link, embed it on your site, or use available integrations, and responses will start appearing in Typeform's Results section.
